title: Project Overview & What ERDwithAI Does
description: Understand the big picture: ERDwithAI transforms natural language descriptions into production-ready full-stack applications. Learn the end-to-end workflow from business domain to deployed code. This is your north star for understanding why each component exists.

title: Project Structure & Package Organization
description: Get familiar with the monorepo layout. Understand the four main packages: @erdwithai/core (business logic), @erdwithai/ai (AI agents), @erdwithai/generator (code generation), and @erdwithai/web (frontend). See how they depend on each other and what each one does.

title: Core Foundation: Types & Entity Model
description: Master the fundamental data structures. Learn about EntityDefinition, EntityAttribute, Relationship, and Zod schemas. These types flow through every other layer, so understanding them deeply is critical. This is the common language that all components speak.

title: Hook System: Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: Learn how the hook system provides validation, audit trails, and business rules at entity lifecycle events. Understand the registry, executor, and builder pattern. This is the mechanism that makes BaseService powerful and extensible.

title: Service Layer: BaseService & Database
description: Understand the abstract BaseService class that provides CRUD operations with integrated hook execution. Learn how services are the backbone that orchestrates hooks, workflows, and database operations. Then explore the Kysely database service for type-safe queries.

title: Core Foundations Summary: Running Locally
description: Before moving to AI agents, get the core packages working locally. Run the database service, understand schema, and see hooks in action. This gives you the foundation for understanding everything downstream.

title: AI Agent Pipeline: From Words to Entities
description: Learn the sequential AI agent pipeline that converts natural language into structured entities. Follow the flow: domain-agent (analyzes business) → entity-agent (refines structure) → relationship-agent (finds connections) → mermaid-agent (generates diagram syntax). Each agent passes output to the next.

title: AI Converter: Orchestrating the Pipeline
description: See how AIToMermaidConverter orchestrates all agents and handles the complete transformation. Understand error handling, validation, and the fallback converter. This is the entry point that ties all agents together.

title: Mastra.ai Configuration & Code Agent
description: Understand how Mastra.ai framework configures AI agents, storage, logging, and observability. Learn about the code-agent for code generation with E2B sandbox integration. This is the AI runtime infrastructure.

title: Code Generation: The Orchestrator
description: Learn the multi-stage code generation process. Understand the GeneratorOrchestrator that coordinates dictionary generation, Mermaid parsing, and full-stack code generation. See how it orchestrates all the downstream generators.

title: Dictionary Generation: Compiere-Style Metadata
description: Understand the DictionaryGenerator that creates sys_* metadata tables following Compiere ERP patterns. This metadata-driven approach enables runtime flexibility and schema evolution. Learn why this is powerful for generated applications.

title: Stack-Specific Generators: TanStack Start & NestJS
description: Dive into full-stack code generation. Learn how TanStackStartFrontendGenerator creates React components and routes, and how NestJSBackendGenerator creates API controllers and services. See how both extend BaseGenerator with stack-specific Handlebars templates.

title: Code Generation Pipeline: End-to-End
description: Walk through a complete code generation example. See how Mermaid syntax becomes Entity objects, how Dictionary metadata is generated, and how frontend and backend code is generated from templates. Run bun run generate:tanstack to see it in action.

title: Frontend: TanStack Start Fundamentals
description: Learn TanStack Start routing differences from Next.js. Understand file-based routing with $ prefixes for dynamic segments, createFileRoute() for pages, and createAPIFileRoute() for API endpoints. This is your entry point to the web application architecture.

title: Frontend: Project Wizard & Navigation Flow
description: Understand the multi-step project wizard UI: projects list → init → design → generate → enhance → deploy. Learn how each step connects to the backend AI services and code generation. See the project flow from user's perspective.

title: Frontend: ERD Design Step & Human-In-The-Loop
description: Deep dive into the design.tsx page. Learn how the visual ERD editor works with Mermaid rendering, entity approval workflow, and version management. This is where humans validate the AI's understanding before code generation.

title: Frontend: API Endpoints for AI Conversion
description: Learn the API routes that power the frontend: convert.ts (natural language to Mermaid), generate.ts (triggering code generation), and deploy.ts (deployment). These are the bridges between frontend UI and backend AI/generation services.

title: Full End-to-End Flow: Natural Language to Application
description: Trace a complete user journey: user enters natural language → API calls convert.ts → AIToMermaidConverter runs agent pipeline → Mermaid ERD displayed → user approves → generate.ts called → GeneratorOrchestrator generates full-stack code → TanStack/NestJS app created. Understand all the moving pieces working together.

title: Testing: Unit Tests with Vitest
description: Learn how to write and run unit tests. Understand the test setup, jsdom environment, and how to test core services, agents, and generators. Run bun run test to see coverage. This ensures code quality across the system.

title: Testing: E2E Tests with Playwright
description: Learn how to write Playwright E2E tests that exercise the full UI flow. Understand the test structure, base URL setup, and how to test complete workflows. Run bun run test:playwright to validate the entire application.

title: Development Workflow: Build, Type-Check, Lint
description: Master the development commands: bun run build (compile all packages), bun run type-check (TypeScript validation), bun run lint (ESLint), bun run format (Prettier). These are run before every commit to maintain code quality.

title: Running the Full Stack Locally
description: Get both services running: Terminal 1 runs bun run dev for the web app (port 3000), Terminal 2 runs bun run dev:mastra for the Mastra service (port 4111). Test the complete system end-to-end locally before deploying.

title: Rules Engine & Business Logic
description: Learn the RulesEngineService that integrates GoRules zen-engine for JDM (Java Decision Model) evaluation. Understand how business rules can be attached to entities and executed during lifecycle events using the hook system.

title: Architecture Decisions & Design Patterns
description: Read the DESIGN.md document to understand key architecture decisions: why hooks, why dictionary metadata, why Mastra.ai, why Kysely. Understand the tradeoffs made and the philosophy behind the system design.

title: Next Steps: Contributing & Extending
description: Now that you understand the system, learn how to contribute: adding new agents, creating generator templates, adding API routes, and writing tests. Check TODOS.md for current work items and opportunities to extend the system.
